Title: Mercy Malaysia calls for stronger NGO governance, transparency reforms
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Mercy Malaysia has called for stronger governance, better regulations and greater transparency of Malaysian non-governmental organisations (NGO) amid growing scrutiny over accountability and fund management.
Its president Datuk Dr Ahmad Faizal Mohd Perdaus said the humanitarian sector could not afford to lose public trust following recent allegations and investigations involving humanitarian and humanitarian-leaning organisations.
He said the concerns prompted Mercy Malaysia to organise the Governance and Accountability Workshop 2026 at its headquarters here today.
"Humanitarian organisations operate on the foundation of public trust.
"Strengthening governance and accountability is not only an institutional responsibility, but also a moral and ethical obligation to the communities we serve, our partners and the wider public," he said during a press conference.
Ahmad Faizal said one of the sector's biggest challenges was Malaysia's fragmented NGO regulatory framework.
"There are many ways to set up and register an NGO in Malaysia. There are different regulatory bodies looking at slightly different types of organisations.
"Which means there are also different laws and regulations governing these organisations," he said.
He added that existing regulations also lacked clear rules governing investments by not-for-profit organisations.
Life Care International Medical Group chief executive officer Datuk Dr Aliyah Karen said the large number of regulatory bodies overseeing NGOs had created inconsistencies in governance requirements.
"When you set up a foundation, you have too many platforms. If you have five ministries governing NGOs, I think that's a lot.
"Everyone has a different set of rules," she said.
Aliyah said NGOs should no longer be treated as informal entities operating outside professional standards.
"NGOs are not poor. Many are doing fantastic work out there and they need funds to support their operations.
"The issue is because of a few NGOs that have taken governance, transparency and accountability for granted," she said.
She added that NGOs should be managed with the same level of professionalism as corporate organisations, including stronger reporting and transparency practices.
Meanwhile, Centre of Excellence in Islamic Social Finance at INCEIF University director Prof Dr Amir Shaharuddin said governance challenges were further complicated by differing regulations governing Islamic social finance mechanisms such as zakat and wakaf.
"For example, some allow a five per cent management fee while others allow up to 20 per cent. It is not standardised," he said.
Ahmad Faizal said public scepticism and donor concerns had increased following recent controversies involving NGOs, although Mercy Malaysia itself had not been implicated.
"There have been more questions asked. We have seen a dip in general public donations.
"But this is not just about one or two NGOs. It affects the whole sector," he said.
He also cautioned against using governance concerns as justification to restrict civil society space in Malaysia.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia managing global supply crisis on four fronts, says Economy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The government will address the ongoing global supply crisis through four key priorities, namely securing essential supplies, optimising existing stock, curbing price increases, and capitalising on new energy opportunities, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said.
Akmal added that the four priorities would guide the government's response as global pressures mounted.
"We cannot fully control global oil prices or geopolitical conflicts. However, we can control national preparedness, policy precision, effectiveness of implementation and fairness in channelling assistance. That is the government's focus at this time," he said.
He was speaking during q briefing on the global supply crisis following the 11th National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting of 2026 here today.
Akmal said the first priority was securing supply, particularly of food, energy, medicine, and essential goods. The second was extending and optimising existing supplies through subsidy management, leakage control, and enforcement.
The third was curbing price increases to ensure that cost pressures were not passed on in full to the public. The fourth was capitalising on new opportunities, including in biodiesel, sustainable fuel, and alternative energy sources.
Akmal reiterated that Malaysia's economy remained on a solid footing despite the challenging external environment, with GDP growth in the first quarter of 2026 coming in at 5.4 per cent, exceeding the earlier preliminary estimate of 5.3 per cent.
The performance was driven by resilient domestic demand, a strong services and manufacturing sector, increased private investment, and robust tourist arrivals.
He said the figure compared favourably with several major economies that had also reported first-quarter GDP results, including Singapore at 4.6 per cent, the Philippines at 2.8 per cent, and China at 5.0 per cent, though Malaysia trailed Vietnam at 7.8 per cent and Indonesia at 5.6 per cent.
MTEN today also heard a presentation from the Federation of Malaysian Manufacturers (FMM) on the impact of the energy crisis and global supply disruption on the manufacturing sector.
A survey of 225 companies across 29 manufacturing subsectors found that pressures were widespread, with the five most affected subsectors being automotive and components, food and beverages, plastics and packaging, chemicals and petrochemicals, and electrical and electronics.
Akmal said the government was particularly concerned about small and medium enterprises, which had more limited financial capacity to absorb prolonged cost pressures.
A RM5 billion SME Stabilisation Relief Facility had been made available through Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) to provide working capital financing to affected SMEs, while financing guarantee support was also available through Syarikat Jaminan Pembiayaan Perniagaan Berhad.
MTEN also reviewed proposed improvements to the Subsidised Diesel Control System with the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry to follow up on adjustments to subsidy quotas aimed at tightening leakage controls without disrupting business operations and supply chains.
Akmal said MTEN had convened 11 times during the crisis period, reviewing 24 papers across 13 economic sectors and recording 96 decisions, of which 11 had been completed and 78 were being implemented.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Colorado reports hantavirus death unrelated to cruise outbreak
Article:
WASHINGTON: An adult in Colorado has died after a confirmed case of hantavirus that is not linked to a recent outbreak aboard a cruise ship in the Atlantic, Colorado Department of Public Health and Environment officials said in a statement.
The strain of hantavirus that caused the Colorado death occurs regularly in Colorado at this time of year, the statement said, and officials are investigating the source of exposure.
Hantavirus is primarily spread by rodents but can be transmitted between people ​in rare cases and after prolonged, close contact. Incubation can last about six weeks.
The Dutch-flagged MV Hondius cruise docked at Rotterdam on Monday. It had been ​carrying around 150 passengers and crew from ⁠23 countries when a cluster of severe respiratory illnesses was first reported to the World Health Organization on May 2.
Three people on the liner have died. Including those deaths, there have been eight confirmed and two probable cases on board, according to WHO.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMA calls for immediate liquid nicotine relisting under Poisons Act following court ruling
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Medical Association (MMA) has called for the immediate relisting of liquid nicotine under the Poisons Act 1952 following a recent landmark High Court ruling.
The High Court recently ruled that the 2023 delisting of liquid nicotine from the Poisons List was
unlawful
.
MMA welcomed the decision, stating it had maintained its opposition since the delisting was gazetted in March 2023.
Its president, Datuk Dr Thirunavukarasu Rajoo, said the association had repeatedly warned against the move since March 2023.
"We warned that removing nicotine from the Poisons Act before any tobacco control law was in place was reckless," he said in a statement today.
He said MMA had urged the prime minister to reverse the decision and expressed disappointment when the advice was ignored.
Thirunavukarasu added that the Poisons Board had given unanimous expert advice, which was ultimately overruled.
"The court has confirmed that it was wrong," he said, noting the case highlighted concerns over health policy being shaped by fiscal considerations rather than medical evidence.
The association urged the government not to appeal the ruling, but to accept and learn from it.
"No future public health decision should be made primarily for fiscal reasons. The health of our children is not a budget line item.
"The Poisons Board exists for a reason. So does the rule of law," he said.
MMA called for liquid nicotine to be relisted under the Poisons Act without delay and that expert medical advice be respected in future public health decisions.
The
issue
comes amid ongoing legal and regulatory developments involving the Health Ministry and the Attorney-General's Chambers over the judicial review of nicotine's exemption from the Poisons Act.
The NST has reached out to the Health Ministry for comment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Not betrayal': Johor BN defends decision to contest alone
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Barisan Nasional's (BN) decision to contest the next Johor state election on its own cannot be deemed a betrayal of Pakatan Harapan (PH), says Johor Umno liaison committee deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan.
He said the PH-BN cooperation only came into existence after the 15th General Election in November 2022.
"After five days without a government, on Nov 24, 2022, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim took his oath after Umno and BN agreed to support him.
"When BN won 40 out of 56 seats in the March 2022 Johor state election, there was no unity government yet. Both BN and PH contested all 56 seats.
"In this state election, BN did not betray anyone.
"If in March 2022, we were with PH, then it would be betrayal.
"We are just repeating what happened before," he told the New Straits Times, echoing remarks by coalition secretary-general
Datuk Seri Zambry Abdul Kadir
earlier.
The remarks followed criticism from PH leaders over
BN's decision to contest all 56 seats
in the next Johor state election on its own.
Johor BN had also rejected claims that its decision
amounted to a betrayal of PH.
Tensions between PH and BN have also intensified following the Negri Sembilan political crisis, which saw 14 BN assemblymen withdraw support for Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zambry: No way... they're the ones who should adapt to our education system
Article:
KUALA BERANG: The government will not bow to pressure from any group seeking to change the core entry requirements for public universities for their own interests, said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abd Kadir.
"If they want us to change it for their own interests, 'no way'. They are the ones who must adapt to our education system," he said.
He said the core policy for entry into public universities will still be based on the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia examination, even if multiple parties criticise it.
He added that the decision had been finalised at the cabinet level and serves as the primary basis for student admission system to public universities via UPUOnline.
"As long as I am the minister, I will not allow higher education without SPM as the foundation.
"It does not matter whether someone studied at this school or that school; the basic requirement remains that they must have an SPM qualification," he told reporters after launching the Higher Education Tour Programme @ Terengganu (PKPT@T) and the Madani Adopted Village Programme at SMK Menerong.
Present were Higher Education director-general Professor Datuk Dr Azlinda Azman, Universiti Malaysia Terengganu vice-chancellor Professor Dr Mohd Zamri Ibrahim and Central Terengganu Development Authority (Ketengah) chairman Datuk Rozi Mamat.
Zambry said the national education system has a clear structure with distinct roles between the Education Ministry, which manages schools, and the Higher Education Ministry, which handles university admissions.
He added that this difference in functions must be understood so that no confusion arises regarding the policy for student admission into public universities.
"The UPUOnline system was developed with a standardized evaluation framework to ensure that all students are assessed fairly based on their SPM results.
"The government will not open up avenues for admission into public universities based on certificates or alternative qualifications other than SPM as the primary basis," he said.
Zambry also dismissed allegations the policy discriminates against any type of education system, including religious schools and traditional pondok institutions.
He said all students, no matter their background, are encouraged to take SPM to qualify themselves for higher learning.
He said certain groups may have misunderstood the policy, mistakenly believing that it is unfair to certain types of education systems.
He added that it was unfair to directly compare the schooling system with the university intake system as the two systems operate differently.
He referred to previous explanations that students from religious schools have the same opportunity to pursue further education, provided they meet the established basic requirements.
Zambry said the SPM foundation not only serves as an academic benchmark but also ensures uniformity in the student selection process for public universities.
He said the system has been in use for a long time and is proven to help ensure that the student admission process runs in an organized and transparent manner.
Previously, the Higher Education Ministry had announced tthat the cabinet had agreed on several new resolutions regarding the admission pathway for school-leavers outside the national education system, including those from tahfiz schools or institutions, private schools, and Chinese independent high schools.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Southeast Asia's e-commerce market projected to reach US$289.8bil by 2029
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Southeast Asia's e-commerce trade is on a strong growth trajectory, set to become the world's second fastest-growing e-commerce market, with a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 13.2 per cent from 2024 to 2029.
According to a leading regional payment platform in Southeast Asia, 2C2P by Antom, the region's e-commerce market is expected to grow by 85.4 per cent to reach US$289.8 billion by 2029.
"Digital payments are projected to account for 97 per cent of total e-commerce transactions by 2029, up from 89 per cent in 2024.
"The strongest growth is expected in domestic payment methods, namely instant payments and local bank-based payment schemes, as well as mobile wallets, particularly in markets such as Indonesia, Thailand and Vietnam," it said in a report titled 'How Southeast Asia Buys and Pays 2026: Unlocking SMEs' Potential'.
The report said growth in digital payments across Southeast Asia's e-commerce market between 2024 and 2029 is expected to encompass several payment methods, including a 104 per cent increase in domestic payments, which are projected to rise to US$92 billion in 2029 from US$45.1 billion in 2024.
Additionally, the growth in digital payments will also be driven by mobile wallets, which are expected to reach US$79.0 billion in 2029 from US$38.2 billion in 2024, while Buy Now Pay Later (BNPL) transactions are projected to surge 174 per cent to US$18.9 billion from US$6.9 billion over the same period.
The report also emphasised that small and medium enterprises (SMEs) are the key pillar driving Southeast Asia's economic growth and are becoming increasingly active in digital commerce. However, they are still underserved in payment and e-commerce analysis.
2C2P by Antom chief executive officer, Worachat Luxkanalode said business in Southeast Asia, particularly SMEs, are the backbone of the region's economic growth, contributing more than 50 per cent to gross domestic product in key markets and employing 64.6 per cent of the workforce.
"As payment ecosystems evolve rapidly across markets, businesses need solutions that simplify operations, support multiple local payment methods and enable cross-border expansion.
"With our business-focused payment platform, companies of all sizes can address these challenges through a single Application Programming Interface, unlocking new opportunities and participating fully in the region's fast-growing digital economy," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Woman stabbed in neck stable after 10-hour surgery
Article:
ALOR STAR: A woman who was stabbed in the neck inside a car outside a restaurant in Titi Gajah, Kepala Batas, has undergone a successful 10-hour surgery at Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB).
The victim's sister said the operation began at 9am and concluded at 7pm yesterday, adding that the victim remains under treatment in the intensive care unit.
"My sister is currently intubated and sedated so she can rest," she said when met at the family home here today, while requesting privacy as the family was not ready to issue further statements.
She said the family is looking after the victim's two children while she recovers in the hospital.
Meanwhile, a neighbour in Taman Wira, Mergong, expressed shock after hearing about the incident.
The neighbour said the victim lived there with her two children but was rarely at home and that they had never spoken.
Kota Setar district police chief Assistant Commissioner Syed Basri Syed Ali said the victim is currently in stable condition.
It was previously reported that the incident occurred at 9.19pm on Sunday, and the case is being investigated under Section 326 of the Penal Code.
The 32-year-old
suspect was arrested
while hiding in a mosque area in Taman Uda, more than four hours after fleeing the scene.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Velesto Energy's net profit falls to RM27.85mil in Q1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Velesto Energy Bhd's net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 2026) declined to RM27.85 million versus RM52.60 million a year ago.
Revenue fell to RM183.35 million against RM224.64 million previously, dragged down by lower average daily charter rates during the quarter.
Its drilling services segment's revenue fell to RM179.9 million from RM221.1 million in Q1 2025, mainly attributed to a lower average daily charter rate (Q1 2026: US$108,000 per day; Q1 2025: US$127,000 per day).
"However, this was partly offset by an improvement in utilisation, which increased to 75 per cent in the current quarter under review from 67 per cent in Q1 2025," it said in a filing with Bursa Malaysia today.
Velesto has also announced an interim dividend for the financial year 2026 (FY2026) of 75 sen per share, a payout amounting to RM62 million, to be paid on Aug 17, 2026 to shareholders registered at the close of business on July 20, 2026.
Its president, Megat Zariman Abdul Rahim said the latest performance reflected a disciplined quarter with higher utilisation and perfect asset uptime, despite the lower daily charter rates.
"We anticipate potentially tighter rig market conditions, driven by a renewed focus on energy security and higher capital expenditure by oil and gas operators in the region.
"In light of this, we will continue maximising opportunities for our own rigs while pursuing additional contracts through strategic collaborations to expand market share and enhance earnings opportunities," he said.
He added that recent awarded NAGA 2 long-term contract with Petronas Carigali Sdn Bhd strengthens the group's earnings visibility.
Velesto has also secured a contract from Hibiscus Oil and Gas Malaysia, marking its first jack-up rig arrangement.
As at April 2026, Velesto's order book stood at RM1.1 billion, supported by a healthy tender book of RM3.1 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Inmate jailed 20 months for fracturing cellmate's eye socket over dirty toilet
Article:
MUAR: A drug detainee was sentenced to 20 months' jail by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to punching a fellow inmate and fracturing his eye socket during a fight at the Muar Correctional Centre two months ago.
Police investigations showed that the fight broke out because the accused lost his temper after the victim neglected toilet cleanliness.
Magistrate Kartini Kasran meted out the sentence against Mohammad Dzaki Robani, 32, after considering his plea, mitigation, and submissions from both the prosecution and defence.
According to the charge sheet, the accused, who is single, was charged with causing grievous hurt to a 33-year-old male detainee at the correctional centre at 2.30pm on March 5.
The offence was framed under Section 325 of the Penal Code, which provides for a maximum prison sentence of seven years and a fine upon conviction.
Based on the facts of the case, a prison warden was alerted to a fight involving two inmates at the centre.
Investigations revealed that the accused punched the victim once in the face, causing severe injuries.
The victim was rushed to the Sultanah Fatimah Specialist Hospital here and had to undergo surgery for a fractured eye socket bone.
The incident occurred near the detention centre's pond area and was witnessed by two supervisors, who later lodged a police report.
Deputy public prosecutor Yasmin Mohd Piah prosecuted, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer Tee Xi Ran.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lorry driver pleads not guilty to assaulting stepdaughter
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A lorry driver was charged at the Yong Peng magistrate's court here today with allegedly assaulting his 12-year-old stepdaughter.
The accused pleaded not guilty after the charges were read before magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
He is alleged to have caned and slapped the victim for disciplinary reasons, causing injuries on 2 occasions in April and again in May.
The case was framed under Section 323 of the Penal Code for causing hurt, read together with Section 326A for domestic abuse.
Section 323 provides for a jail term of up to one year, a fine, or both, while Section 326A carries enhanced penalties for domestic-related offences.
Based on a police report lodged by the victim's mother, who is also the accused's wife, the child was allegedly caned and slapped for disciplinary reasons.
The incident is said to have caused injuries to the victim's cheek and legs.
Police later arrested the accused and seized a cane believed to have been used in the incident.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while the accused was represented by National Legal Aid Foundation lawyer Tan Song Yan.
Earlier, the prosecution requested a mention date and proposed bail of RM12,000 for all charges.
The defence, however, sought a lower bail amount, citing the accused's monthly income of RM1,700.
Following mitigation, the court set bail at RM3,000 for all charges. The accused failed to post bail and was sent to the Muar Correctional Centre pending the next court date.
The court fixed June 24 for mention and document submission.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Petronas Gas's Q1 net profit slips 6.4pct to RM438.7mil
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Gas Bhd's net profit fell 6.4 per cent to RM438.7 million in the first quarter of financial year 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM468.8 million a year ago.
The lower net profit was mainly due to weaker revenue from the utilities segment and higher operating expenses.
This was driven by increased depreciation across all segments as well as higher repair and maintenance activities in the gas processing segment.
Petronas Gas' revenue slipped 0.6 per cent to RM1.58 billion from RM1.59 billion in Q1 2025.
The company declared a dividend of 16 sen per share that will be paid June 23.
Petronas Gas said its outlook for 2026 is expected to remain resilient despite margin pressures, particularly in the utilities segment, amid higher fuel gas prices driven by ongoing global geopolitical uncertainties.
"Contributions from the gas transportation, regasification and gas processing segments are expected to continue providing earnings stability, underpinned by the regulated framework and stable long-term contracts.
"However, the group remains attentive to potential indirect impacts from geopolitical developments, including inflationary pressures and supply chain disruptions.
"Petronas Gas remains committed to strengthening operational resilience, commercial excellence and cost optimisation, while continuing to pursue long-term strategic growth to sustain earnings quality for all shareholders," it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malayan Flour to build Vietnam plant as it spends RM100mil on capacity expansion, automation
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malayan Flour Mills Bhd (MFM) has allocated RM100 million in capital expenditure (capex) over the next two financial years to increase its daily flour milling capacity in Vietnam and drive automation in Malaysia.
Of the total capex, RM80 million will be used to build a new milling line at Vimaflour Ltd in Vietnam.
MFM executive deputy chairman cum managing director Teh Wee Chye said the expansion will increase milling capacity in the country to 2,500 tonnes per day from 2,000 tonnes per day to meet rising regional demand, as existing operations are already running at full capacity
On why a larger allocation was directed towards Vietnam, Teh said Vietnam's economy is expanding rapidly and continues to show a tremendous appetite for growth.
"They are one of the fastest growing economies in Asia now. As the economy grows, the gross domestic product grows, people will eat better quality food and more nutritious food," he said at MFM's press conference after its annual general meeting.
The remaining RM20 million capex will be allocated for upgrades, automation and other operational improvements across its flour milling operations in Malaysia and Vietnam.
"Part of the planned capex includes also investing in automation for our 25kg and 1kg flour bagging operations. The next stage in Malaysia, we will explore capacity expansion possibly by adding another 400 tonnes at our existing operations, which have been running for the past 40 to 50 years." Teh said.
The investment is in line with the growth momentum in the company's flour business, he said.
He added that the expansion will enhance production capacity and position it to better meet rising regional demand going forward.
"We believe continued investments across our integrated value chain are important to enhance long-term operational resilience and reinforce the group's position as a reliable food security player in the region," he added.
The capex will be funded through internally generated funds as well as bank borrowings.
On developments in the Middle East, Teh said wheat futures prices have risen mainly due to adverse weather conditions in the US, the Middle East crisis and higher fertiliser prices.
He added that if the conflict were to persist, fertiliser prices could continue to increase and further impact farmers.
"Fertiliser prices have already risen by 50 per cent to 100 per cent. If prices do not increase sufficiently to offset the higher fertiliser costs, some farmers may choose not to continue planting, and this is something beyond our control," he said.
On its ready-to-eat (RTE) business, Teh said the company has received encouraging response and feedback, particularly from younger consumers.
He added that the company is currently in the process of designing and constructing a new plant to expand production capacity.
"The RTE business contribution is still very small, but we see strong growth potential in this segment.
"Currently, we are producing further processed products such as chicken nuggets and frankfurters, and we only began developing the RTE business around eight to nine months ago.
"We are now in the process of building a new plant, and it will take about a year or so before the products are fully rolled out," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RHB Research upgrades 99 Speed Mart to 'Buy'
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Research has upgraded its call for 99 Speed Mart Retail Holdings Bhd to "Buy" on the back of the company's improved financial performance for the first quarter (Q1).
The firm said that 99 Speed Mart's results met expectations underpinned by an exciting sales momentum and disciplined cost control.
The firm kept its target price on the stock at RM4.02.
"Valuation is looking more palatable now given 99 Speed Mart's resilient fundamentals to offer earnings visibility amidst the ongoing market challenges. The promising earnings growth will be supported by the increased budget allocation for Sumbangan Asas Rahmah (SARA) and robust outlet expansion to deepen market penetration ," it said in a note.
99 Speed Mart's posted a higher net profit of RM188.56 million in the first quarter of its financial year ended March 31 (Q1 2026), compared with RM144.90 million in the same period last year.
It said the performance was driven by an improved non-essential product mix and effective energy-saving measures.
Revenue jumped to RM3.07 billion in Q1 2026 compared to RM2.61 billion in Q1 2025.
On the implications of the Middle East conflict, RHB Research said management has neither observed nor foreseen any material cost pressures and supply chain disruptions in the immediate term.
"That said, any cost increases will be passed on to consumers to conserve its gross profit margin. Meanwhile, the rising electricity tariff is mitigated by optimisation efforts whilst the spike in diesel expenses is covered by the government's subsidy programme," it said.
As at 3pm, 99 Speed Mart's share price rose 4.57 per cent or 15 sen to RM3.43, valuing the company at RM28.2 billion. More than 7 million shares changed hands.
The counter was among the top three gainers on Bursa Malaysia today.
The stock climbed as much as 6.4 per cent to hit an intraday high of RM3.49, versus its closing price of RM3.28 on Monday.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Ex-Perlis MB's son acquitted of RM19,505 false MACC claim charge
Article:
KANGAR: The Sessions Court here today acquitted and discharged the son of former Perlis menteri besar Mohd Shukri Ramli from a charge of submitting a false claim of RM19,505.10.
Judge Datuk Nu'aman Mahmud Zuhudi ruled that the prosecution had failed to establish a prima facie case against Mohd Syafeeq Mohd Shukri, 37, after examining witness testimony.
As such, the accused was not called to enter his defence.
"Therefore, the accused is acquitted and discharged, and the bail money of RM20,000 is to be returned," Nu'aman said.
On May 23, 2024, Syafeeq pleaded not guilty to a charge of submitting a government order to Nurul Nabilah Mohd Sukri, an administrative assistant at the District Administration Division of the Perlis State Secretary's Office, with the intent to deceive.
He was accused of having reason to believe that the claim document dated Feb 14, 2024, in the name of Mohd Farid Abdul Hamid, contained false particulars.
The claim was related to beverage supply services for the Raja of Perlis, Tuanku Syed Sirajuddin Jamalullail, at Tuanku Fauziah Hospital here.
The charge was brought under Section 18 of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009.
MACC deputy public prosecutor Ahmad Fariz Abdul Hamid said 11 prosecution witnesses were called over the trial period. He added that the prosecution will file a notice of appeal with the High Court within 14 days.
Syafeeq was represented by lawyer Mohd Fadhly Yaacob.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia Marine's earnings rise 17pct on stronger heavy engineering contributions
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia Marine and Heavy Engineering Holdings Bhd (MMHE) posted a 17 per cent increase in net profit to RM14.3 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26) from RM12 million a year ago, supported by stronger contributions from its heavy engineering segment.
MMHE's revenue rose 15.2 per cent to RM521.9 million from RM453.1 million last year.
In line with the higher revenue, the company recorded an operating profit of RM18.1 million, up 17.5 per cent from RM15.4 million previously.
MMHE said the outlook for 2026 continues to be influenced by heightened geopolitical tensions in the Middle East, which are creating trade and economic uncertainties.
The group said these conditions are expected to keep energy prices elevated, disrupt supply chains and dampen global investment sentiment across the energy and marine sectors.
"Despite these challenges, growing demand for offshore floater conversions and liquefied natural gas (LNG) vessel repairs is expected to create opportunities for the group," it said.
MMHE added that its heavy engineering segment will continue focusing on project delivery excellence.
This includes safe and timely execution, while adopting a selective approach across both conventional and emerging energy sectors in domestic and international markets to maintain a balanced portfolio.
Meanwhile, the marine segment is expected to continue contributing steadily, supported by the company's expertise in LNG repairs and floater conversions, as well as continued demand for repair, maintenance and conversion works.
MMHE said ongoing yard modernisation efforts are expected to enhance operational capabilities and improve its ability to deliver more competitive solutions to clients, supporting sustainable long-term growth.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zara's mum did not want a post-mortem, court hears
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: Noraidah Lamat did not want a post-mortem to be conducted on the late Zara Qairina Mahathir, the Coroner's Court heard today.
Nur Shira Abdullah said her sister-in-law had told her privately in a hospital ward when there were no visitors present.
"Zara's mother had said that she did not want a post-mortem as she did not want her child to be 'hurt' or 'cut open'.
"She told me this in the ward. We never discussed the issue of a post-mortem in public," Nur Shira, the 71st witness testified before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 69th day of the inquest into Zara Qairina's death.
The 13-year-old was pronounced dead on July 17, a day after she was found unconscious on the ground floor of the SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha dormitory.
Nur Shira, 46, was examined by lawyer Shahlan Jufri on statements by teachers who had previously testified that they overheard "Zara's aunt had refused a post-mortem and Zara's grandfather agreed as he did not want the child to be cut open."
Shahlan is representing Noraidah.
Nur Shira, who is from Kuala Lumpur, also said she could not communicate with the grandfather due to a language barrier, and therefore found it strange that he would have spoken about the matter.
She also denied that they had been provided with an explanation by a medical doctor regarding the importance of a post-mortem.
Meanwhile, in reply to Shahlan regarding the school's request for Zara Qairina's body to be brought to SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha for final respects, Nur Shira said it was a suggestion made by the school principal through an ustazah.
"I told my husband that our child (Zara Qairina) was not a kitten to be moved around for display.
"If they want to pay their last respects, they should rent a bus and go to Sipitang."
The inquest will resume at 2pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Dennis Lim named new Penang police chief in police reshuffle
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Federal police Criminal Investigation Department (CID) Forensics and Strategic Planning deputy director Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng has been appointed as the new Penang police chief.
Federal police corporate communications chief Assistant Commissioner Julina Salehuddin said Lim will serve as Acting Commissioner of Police, effective June 20.
Johor Baru Selatan district police chief Assistant Commissioner Raub Selamat has been appointed head of the Internal Security and Public Order Department (JKDNKA) in Terengganu.
The current Terengganu JKDNKA chief, Assistant Commissioner Azrul Hisham Mohd Shaffei, has been appointed Johor Baru Selatan district police chief.
"Bandar Baru district police chief in Kedah, Superintendent Syamsul Sinring, has been appointed Kulim district police chief in Kedah," she said.
The transfer also involves the Langkawi Management Division in Kedah, chief Deputy Superintendent Mohamad Sani Mat Ali.
He will be appointed as the new Bandar Baru district police chief with the rank of Acting Superintendent.
Julina said the head of the Klang Selatan Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Division in Selangor, Deputy Superintendent Ramzi Mahamudin, has also been appointed as the new Selama district police chief in Perak with the rank of Acting Superintendent.
The postings will take effect on June 22.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Man apologises after viral video of cat, kittens sparks outrage
Article:
KUANTAN: A trader has come under intense public scrutiny after a split-second decision captured him throwing a box containing a cat and kittens in a manner widely described as heartless.
The posting and screengrabs, which went viral on Facebook, claimed that the 44-year-old's actions had resulted in the deaths of six kittens.
However, Ho Kian Loon dismissed the allegations as untrue, saying he was now caring for the mother cat and all her kittens.
Ho has since issued an apology, expressing deep regret over his actions and insisting that he never intended to hurt the animals.
"I realised that I had done something unacceptable. I acted out of emotion during the incident.
"I did not kill any of the kittens as claimed in the viral postings.
"I take responsibility for my actions. In fact, I never neglected the cats and immediately brought them back to my house and fed them after realising I had made a mistake," he told the New Straits Times when met today.
Ho said he first spotted the mother cat and her kittens inside a large flower pot in front of his shop around noon on May 15 and moved them into a box before leaving it on the walkway.
"When I returned about 6.30pm, I noticed someone had placed the box right in front of my premises. I tried to push the box away, but my actions caused the mother cat to fall out of the box.
"Later, upon realising my mistake, I gathered the kittens, placed them back into the box and kept them inside my shop," he said.
Ho said he was shocked when a friend informed him about a one-minute dashcam video of the incident that went viral on May 17, claiming that his actions had caused the kittens' deaths.
"The posting sparked widespread condemnation on social media, and I am concerned about my family's safety. I lodged a police report on May 17 because the posting did not reflect the truth," he said.
In a Facebook post, Ho admitted his mistake, saying the mother cat and kittens were safe, and expressed hope that the public would forgive him and guide him on how to care for the cats properly.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kerjaya Prospek wins RM174.2mil IJM Land contract in Seremban
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kerjaya Prospek Group Bhd's subsidiary Kerjaya Prospek (M) Sdn Bhd has secured a RM174.2 million contract from IJM Land Bhd's subsidiary RB Land Sdn Bhd for the construction of a high-rise residential development in Seremban, Negri Sembilan.
In a statement, Kerjaya said the contract involves the main building works for a 44-storey serviced apartment block comprising 778 apartment units and 15 retail lots, among others.
Construction is scheduled to commence on June 1, 2026, and is expected to be completed within 35 months.
Kerjaya chief executive officer and executive director Tee Eng Tiong said the contract marked the group's second project secured in Seremban this year, reflecting growing development momentum in Negri Sembilan.
"We are also honoured to forge a new partnership with IJM Land, which underscores the industry's continued confidence in our technical capabilities, execution expertise and unwavering commitment to construction excellence," he added.
Tee said the group remains optimistic on the outlook for the domestic construction sector, supported by sustained demand for strategically located developments and infrastructure expansion.
He added that Kerjaya would continue pursuing high-value projects aligned with its core expertise while maintaining disciplined execution and operational efficiency.
The latest contract win brings Kerjaya's financial year 2026 (FY26) new job wins to RM976.5 million and lifts its outstanding order book to RM4.3 billion.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Navy to identify alternatives for NSM after Norway missile deal falls through
Article:
LUMUT: The Defence Ministry has instructed the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to identify alternatives to replace the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system following procurement complications with Norway.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the matter is being closely reviewed after Norway's decision affected the delivery of the surface-to-surface missile system intended for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) programme.
"Let that be with the Navy (to find the alternatives). We believe the best option is a system that is compatible and can be integrated with the combat system, from a country willing to transfer technology, and from a country we trust and believe is genuinely a partner.
"Sometimes they say they are friends when selling products, but once the purchase is made, they may not be as sincere. All these factors will be considered.
"But the key point now is that we are constrained and may have limited options because the ships are already being built. So we must find a replacement that can be implemented quickly," he said.
He told reporters this after visiting the LCS project at the RMN Naval Shipyard in Lumut today. Also present were Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) chief Admiral Tan Sri Zulhelmy Ithnain and the ministry's secretary-general Datuk Lokman Hakim Ali.
He said the government remains focused on ensuring the LCS vessels are completed according to schedule, despite the setback involving one of the key weapons systems.
He added that the issue only affects the NSM component, while other core weapons systems remain unaffected.
"We are aware that these ships are equipped with four types of weapon systems, namely surface-to-surface, surface-to-air, anti-submarine warfare systems and electronic warfare systems.
"So, the other three weapon systems are not affected; only the NSM is affected. We still have the rest.
"However, the impact is that the absence of this system cannot be replaced immediately because it involves integration with the ship's combat system, followed by testing, training and other related processes. So this is among the areas that will be affected.
"The RMN is working to identify alternatives to replace the Norwegian system, so that eventually these ships will be equipped with the planned weapons systems. So only one system is affected, the other three are fine," he added.
He added that while the ships are expected to be completed according to schedule, the absence of the NSM presents integration and operational challenges.
Khaled also confirmed that progress on all five LCS vessels remains on track, with delivery schedules unchanged.
"As of April, the work progress for each vessel is satisfactory. LCS 1 is at 84 per cent; LCS 2 at 79 per cent; LCS 3 at 71 per cent; LCS 4 at 60 per cent, and LCS 5 at 49 per cent.
"The differences in these figures are due to varying levels of progress for each ship and different phases of construction for each vessel.
"LCS 1 will be delivered in December this year, LCS 2 in August 2027, LCS 3 in December 2027, LCS 4 in August 2028 and LCS 5 in April 2029. That is the delivery schedule for each vessel," he said.
He acknowledged that the NSM issue does have some impact.
"Although the ships may be completed as scheduled, we may be without this system, the Naval Strike Missile. That is the only missing component," he said.
It was reported that Malaysia has issued a notice of demand to a Norwegian defence firm over the cancellation of an export licence for a missile system intended for the LCS, claiming losses exceeding
RM1 billion
.
The government is seeking both direct and indirect costs following Norway's unilateral decision to revoke the export licence for the defence system.
Malaysia had already paid 126 million euros (RM583 million), equivalent to 95 per cent of the contract value, before the cancellation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chow: Penang '75pct prepared' if snap polls called
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: Penang is "75 per cent prepared" to face a snap general election should Parliament be dissolved unexpectedly.
Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow, however, stressed that the state government's focus remained on delivering its 2023 unity government manifesto rather than election preparations.
"I always give the answer 75 per cent. Not completely prepared, but adequately prepared if there is a possibility," he told reporters after presenting the latest progress report on the state manifesto at his office in Komtar today.
Chow had recently downplayed speculation over Pakatan Harapan's electoral prospects in the state, describing the political landscape ahead of the next general election as a "numbers game".
He said it was still too early to make firm predictions, but acknowledged that
no single party or coalition
would be able to form the government on its own.
Chow said Penang had achieved more than a 90 per cent implementation rate for manifesto pledges categorised as either completed or ongoing.
Based on data as of April 30, 30 out of 50 manifesto promises, or 60 per cent, had been fulfilled, while another 17 promises, equivalent to 34 per cent, were being implemented.
Only three initiatives have yet to begin.
"We consider that the manifesto offered during the election must be implemented and realised for the well-being of all Penangites," he said.
Chow said the state government had developed the My Penang Manifesto dashboard through collaboration between the Chief Minister's Office, the state Economic Planning Unit and the state Information and Communications Technology Unit to ensure transparent monitoring of every pledge.
"The use of this dashboard ensures that no initiative is left behind and every sen of public allocation is utilised according to its intended target," he said.
Among the three initiatives yet to be implemented are the establishment of a State Social Development Fund financed through medical health fees imposed on foreign nationals, a dedicated rental housing scheme for youths and singles, and the setting up of kiriai ceremonial centres in every district together with the construction of a new Tamil school.
Explaining the delay, Chow said some projects required longer implementation periods and might not be completed within a single term.
"But at least for long-term projects, we will strive to launch them," he said.
On political cooperation ahead of the next general election, Chow, who is also Penang Pakatan Harapan chairman, said the coalition's ties with Barisan Nasional in the state remained open despite recent developments in Johor and Negeri Sembilan.
He said any final decision on electoral cooperation would ultimately be determined by the national leadership.
"Ultimately, the decision comes from the leadership at the national level and all the states, I believe, will have to align themselves with the national decision," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: #NSTviral: Motorists stop female driver after wrong-way travel on Dash highway [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Cars whizzed past as a female driver drove against traffic on the Damansara-Shah Alam Elevated Expressway (Dash), a three-lane dual carriageway in each direction, before motorists intervened to stop the vehicle.
The two-minute clip, shared by the DashCam Malaysia Facebook page, showed the woman driving on the wrong side of the highway as oncoming motorists flashed their headlights.
A motorist in a white car drove alongside the woman's vehicle from the opposite direction, gesturing to alert her that she was on the wrong side of the road, while a motorcyclist on the far right hooted to warn of the danger to herself and other road users.
The vehicle eventually came to a halt.
One user, identified as @Heron5974, commented: "It's time to reanalyse the age limit for driving, too many cases."
Another user, @ZaniZaniy, said authorities should review licences issued to elderly drivers, claiming some were no longer fit to drive safely.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Read, understand Rukun Negara, Selangor ruler tells DAP rep, Ronnie Liu [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has told a DAP assemblyman and a former DAP leader to read the Rukun Negara every week to understand its contents and become courteous citizens who respect the royal institution.
Sultan Sharafuddin said all assemblymen in the state should go to Dataran Selangor twice a month to read the Rukun Negara inscribed on a plaque at the square.
"This is especially the case for Seri Kembangan assemblyman, YB Wong Siew Ki of the DAP and DAP's former state exco, Ronnie Liu.
"I encourage them to go to Dataran Selangor every week to understand and appreciate the Rukun Negara."
[[nid:1443187]]
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 17-year-old school dropout pleads guilty to raping ex-girlfriend
Article:
BATU PAHAT: A 17-year-old school dropout pleaded guilty at the Yong Peng magistrate's court here to raping a student who is also his former girlfriend.
The teenager made the plea after the charge was read under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for statutory rape before magistrate Arun Noval Dass.
According to the charge sheet, he was accused of raping the 15-year-old victim at a house at 3pm in May last year.
Section 376(1) provides for a prison sentence of up to 20 years and whipping, though juvenile offenders may face a good behaviour bond, community service or detention at a Henry Gurney School.
The authorities were alerted by a doctor after the victim disclosed the incident.
Police investigations revealed the incident occurred when the victim travelled to the accused's house using a p-hailing service, where he invited her inside to commit the act.
The victim did not tell anyone about the incident until she revealed it to a medical team while undergoing psychiatric treatment.
Deputy public prosecutor Firdaus Ruslan prosecuted, while the accused was represented by lawyer Tan Song Yan.
The court fixed a later date for the submission of the Welfare Department probation report, facts and sentencing, and granted bail at RM1,000 with one surety.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Affin Bank's momentum seen slowing if uncertainty persists
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Affin Bank Bhd's loan growth momentum remains solid for now but may moderate if geopolitical uncertainties persist beyond June, potentially delaying corporate pipelines and deal conversions.
Hong Leong Investment Bank Bhd (HLIB) said despite near-term headwinds, Affin's net interest margin is expected to recover towards the 1.5 per cent target.
This is supported by ongoing current account and savings account mobilisation, although intense domestic deposit competition is likely to keep funding costs elevated.
The research house said Affin's near-term outlook remains cautious, with its performance highly contingent upon macro and geopolitical stability.
"Asset quality warrants closer monitoring against a backdrop of emerging small and medium enterprise and non-retail stress, keeping provisioning prudent with a financial year 2026 (FY26) credit cost target of 30 basis points.
"That said, earnings visibility should be anchored by non-interest income growth, fuelled by stronger fee income amid structural improvements," it said in a note.
HLIB said after making housekeeping adjustments to FY25 audited figures and revising its margin and provisioning assumptions, it has reduced its FY26 and FY27 earnings forecasts by 2.2 per cent and 12.0 per cent respectively.
"Subsequently, we introduce our FY28 forecasts, implying 10.7 per cent earnings growth," it said.
HLIB said Affin's first-quarter net profit of RM135.5 million was in line with both its and consensus expectations, accounting for 23 per cent of full-year estimates.
The firm maintained its "Buy" call on the bank but lowered its target price to RM3.00 from RM3.20.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PM proud of Malaysia's economic growth despite Middle East conflict [WATCH]
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's economic growth has exceeded projections despite the severe oil price shock triggered by the Middle East crisis, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"Alhamdulillah, despite all that, our economic growth performance last year and in the first quarter of this year has been higher than projected," Anwar said at the National Sports Awards 2025 here today.
Anwar said the achievement was notable given that national economic projections were based on oil trading at around US$70 per barrel. Prices later surged past US$100 per barrel as the conflict escalated.
He said the significant external shock had forced him to cut short his engagements and return to chair economic action meetings.
Anwar added that he was proud of two areas where Malaysia's actual achievements outstripped projections, sports and economic growth, which reflected the country's resilience.
On Saturday, Bank Negara Malaysia governor Datuk Seri Abdul Rasheed Ghaffour said Malaysia's economy is expected to remain resilient in 2026, with
growth sustained
at between four and five per cent despite global uncertainties.
Abdul Rasheed said the economy recorded a strong growth of 5.4 per cent in the first quarter of 2026, driven mainly by domestic demand.
Meanwhile, oil prices fell more than two per cent in early Asian trade today after United States President Donald Trump said he had paused a planned attack on Iran to allow for negotiations to end the Middle East war.
Trump said on Monday he had paused the attack after Iran sent a new peace proposal to Washington.
Tensions in the Middle East intensified following large-scale attacks by the United States and Israel on Iran which began on Feb 28.
In retaliation, Iran targeted US military bases across the region, launched missiles and drones at Israel, and disrupted nearly 20 per cent of global oil and liquefied natural gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kelantan Health Dept warns against frozen food products from Thailand
Article:
KOTA BARU: The Kelantan Health Department has urged parents to monitor their children against purchasing frozen food products from Thailand, especially sausages, due to safety and compliance concerns.
Its director Mohd Azman Yacob said enforcement operations conducted by health officers stationed at Immigration, Customs, Quarantine and Security (ICQS) complexes in the state found that many food items were brought into the country from Sungai Golok, mostly by traders.
"Most of the food products are freshly made before being brought into Malaysia.
"Throughout last year, the most frequently seized food items at the Rantau Panjang ICQS were various types of frozen sausages.
"The food items did not have valid import permits, their halal status could not be verified and they failed to comply with food labelling requirements.
"We do not want children to be exposed to risks from consuming such food," he said.
He added that among other products seized were Thai instant noodles, flavoured bottled drinks, fish crackers and squid soy sauce, which were also found to have labelling issues that did not comply with the Food Regulations 1985.
Dr Mohd Azman said no laboratory tests were conducted on the seized food samples as the manufacturers of the products could not be identified.
On another matter, he said health screening at ICQS entry points would only be carried out when necessary, particularly following disease outbreaks overseas.
"It is not conducted all the time. Screening will only be implemented when there is a need, especially involving travellers showing symptoms at the ICQS complexes.
"Travellers may also be required to complete self-declaration forms depending on the situation and health risks identified," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Aunt breaks down when videos of Zara played in court
Article:
KOTA KINABALU: The Coroner's Court had to stand down proceedings after the aunt of the late Zara Qairina Mahathir broke down in tears when videos of family moments were played.
Lawyer Shahlan Jufri, who is representing Zara Qairina's mother Noraidah Lamat, showed several videos featuring the teenage sharing her school experiences and spending time with her cousins, including recording themselves doing the "Velocity" TikTok trend during Hari Raya.
The 71st witness, Nur Shida Abdullah, said some of the videos were recorded by herself, her second child or by the children themselves either in her living room in Kuala Lumpur or at Zara Qairina's house in Sipitang.
One of the videos also showed the 46-year-old panning her handphone to record the faces of her husband, four children and Zara Qairina while they were sitting at the dining table in her house.
She was heard mentioning each of the children's ages and asking them to remember the moment when the footage was taken.
"They are my small family," she said before coroner Amir Shah Amir Hassan on the 69th day of the inquest into Zara Qairina's death.
The 13-year-old was pronounced dead on July 17, a day after she was found unconscious at the SMKA Tun Datu Mustapha school dormitory.
Nur Shida also broke down when she testified about collecting Zara Qairina's belongings from the school.
She testified that when she, her husband and Noraidah were at the school, none of the teachers apologised or explained what had happened to Zara Qairina.
"They only showed the room where they kept Zara's items.
"Why did they hate Zara to the extent of putting her belongings in black plastic trash bags? These are items for remembrance."
Nur Shida also told the court about mean comments on social media.
Among them was a TikTok account named Plot Twist, which portrayed a negative image of Noraidah and circulated diary contents even before those pieces of evidence were presented during the inquest.
The proceedings will resume at 2pm.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Indonesia signed letter but made no commitment to US on airspace access, says defence minister
Article:
JAKARTA: Indonesia's defence minister said on Tuesday that he had signed a letter of intent last month with US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on granting US military aircraft access to Indonesian airspace, but said no commitment was made.
The letter of intent signed in Washington mentioned respecting each country's territorial integrity, the need for a mechanism if Indonesia agreed to airspace access, and respect for local laws, Defence Minister Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin told a parliamentary hearing.
"We made not a single commitment with the US on airspace. We uphold the constitution and our national interests," Sjafrie said.
The issue of overflight access created controversy in Indonesia last month. The foreign ministry warned the defence ministry that the proposal to give the US military 'blanket' permission to fly over Indonesian territory could risk entangling Jakarta in potential South China Sea conflicts, Reuters reported.
Sjafrie said Hegseth had made the request for the overflight access last year during an Association of Southeast Asian Nations meeting. Hegseth had cited emergency reasons when he asked for the access, Sjafrie said without providing further details.
A request for comment was sent to the Pentagon. There was no immediate reply, and when the request was sent from Jakarta it was early morning Washington time and outside business hours.
After Hegseth and Sjafrie met in Washington last month, the Pentagon said the two countries had established a major defence co-operation partnership and listed a number of ways in which they would build defence ties.
Sjafrie said the partnership includes the modernisation of Indonesia's military, but "it does not mention any military equipment". He said the US would help train Indonesian soldiers.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Economy minister: Govt maintains RM300 diesel aid, adds RM100 interim assistance as global fuel prices rise
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — The government will maintain the RM300 monthly BUDI MADANI Diesel cash assistance while introducing an additional interim RM100 aid payment for eligible recipients amid rising global fuel prices and growing supply chain uncertainty.
Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said the measures were part of Putrajaya’s targeted intervention strategy to shield lower-income groups and vulnerable sectors from the effects of the ongoing global supply crisis.
Announcing the decision after today’s National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting, Akmal Nasrullah said the government remained focused on ensuring the rakyat were not left to bear rising costs alone.
“The government will continue to implement its targeted interventions so that the public will not be affected without the required support,” he said.
He said the continuation of the diesel aid and the additional RM100 interim assistance were aimed at easing cost-of-living pressures linked to volatile global energy markets and higher transportation costs.
Akmal Nasrullah said Malaysia’s economy continued to show resilience despite increasingly uncertain global conditions, with the country recording a stronger-than-expected 5.4 per cent GDP growth in the first quarter of 2026.
He said the growth was supported by resilient domestic demand, strong manufacturing and services sectors, rising private investments and continued foreign tourist arrivals.
The minister noted that Malaysia’s economic performance outpaced several regional economies, including Singapore and the Philippines, although it remained behind Vietnam and Indonesia.
He added that the labour market remained stable, with unemployment holding at 2.9 per cent, indicating continued strength in domestic economic fundamentals.
However, Akmal Nasrullah cautioned that the effects of the global supply crisis were expected to become more pronounced by the third quarter of the year, particularly on prices, industrial operating costs and employment.
He said Brent crude oil prices had risen moderately in recent weeks amid geopolitical uncertainty and concerns over global energy supply stability.
At the same time, inflation increased to 1.9 per cent in April from 1.7 per cent in March, driven largely by transportation costs following higher diesel and RON97 fuel prices.
Akmal Nasrullah said the government was also taking steps to protect food security and agricultural supply chains, including the disbursement of the RM300 per hectare Ploughing Incentive for Farmers beginning today.
He said MTEN had also reviewed proposals to improve the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS), including possible adjustments to diesel subsidy quotas aimed at reducing leakages without disrupting businesses or supply chains.
According to government data, more than 398,000 vehicles had registered under the SKDS programme as of April 9, involving over 375,500 goods transport vehicles and around 23,000 public transport vehicles.
Akmal Nasrullah said the government was also strengthening support for small and medium enterprises (SMEs), particularly those facing prolonged cost pressures from the global energy and supply situation.
Among the measures announced was Bank Negara Malaysia’s RM5 billion SME Stabilisation Relief Facility to help affected SMEs maintain cash flow and operational continuity.
“The government’s approach is not reactive,” he said.
“We are taking action after weeks of monitoring, recalibration and cooperation across ministries and close checks to ensure each decision translates into implementation,” he added.

Title: Zambry: SPM remains main entry requirement for IPTA admissions
Article:
KUALA BERANG, May 19 — Admission to public higher education institutions (IPTA) through the Unit Pusat Universiti (UPU) Online is still based on the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM), said Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir.
He said the government, through a Cabinet decision, has set that the basic requirement for admission to higher education institutions via UPU Online is SPM, regardless of the stream or type of school attended.
“I have previously emphasised that the government and the Cabinet have decided that admission to any IPTA must be based on SPM. It does not matter which school a person attended; admission through UPU remains based on SPM.
“We need to understand that schools are managed by the Ministry of Education Malaysia (MOE), while university admissions are handled through UPU Online. The requirements under UPU Online are based on SPM. We do not base admissions on other results, subjects or certificates,” he said after the launch of the Higher Education Roadshow @Terengganu, held in conjunction with the handing over of Universiti Malaysia Terengganu’s (UMT) Kampung Angkat MADANI here today.
Elaborating further, Zambry Abd Kadir said the ministry does not base university admissions on certificates or systems outside the existing framework.
“To enter UPU and IPTA, the basic requirement (SPM) remains the same. I believe that is the position I have consistently stated, but this issue has become controversial among certain groups, with some feeling that the opportunities provided are still insufficient.
“For me, what is most important is the foundation of our education policy - that entry into university must be based on SPM,” he said.
Commenting on allegations of discrimination against students from religious and pondok schools, he said the government does not discriminate against any group, but instead sets the same basic requirement for all students - namely, sitting for the SPM examination to enable them to pursue tertiary education.
“We do not discriminate against any party. The basis we have set is clear. However, some have portrayed this as a form of manipulation by comparing religious schools with other schools, which is not the case. We are not making comparisons or equating them, as both operate under different systems,” he said.
He added that although there are differing views on the policy, the government stands firm that SPM is the primary basis for university admission in the country. — Bernama

Title: Defence minister: Navy to decide replacement for cancelled Norwegian Naval Strike Missile system
Article:
LUMUT, May 19 —The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has left it to the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) to assess alternatives to replace the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) system, after its supply was cancelled by a Norwegian defence contractor.
Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the move could open the door to cooperation with friendly nations or increased engagement with local defence industry players, in line with efforts to reduce reliance on foreign suppliers.
“Let the Navy decide. We believe the best option is a system that is compatible and can be integrated with the combat system, from countries that are willing to transfer technology and that we trust as genuine partners.
“This (cancelled deal) incident has also strengthened our commitment to enhancing the local defence industry and reducing dependence on external suppliers,” he told reporters after a working visit to inspect progress on the littoral combat ship (LCS) project at Lumut Naval Shipyard at the Navy base here today.
Mohamed Khaled said replacing the NSM system would involve additional costs, including training personnel and integrating a new system with existing platforms.
“The ship has already been fitted with mounting points for the missile. If we procure a different system from another country, the mounting may differ.
“Then there are training costs and the need to ensure integration between the new system and the existing combat system. All of this must be done, or the missile system cannot function effectively,” he said.
However, Mohamed Khaled stressed that the issue would not affect the delivery schedule of the RMN LSCs, which are currently under construction.
He said LCS1 KD Maharajalela is scheduled for delivery in December this year, followed by LCS2 in August 2027, LCS3 in December 2027, LCS4 in August 2028, and LCS5 in April 2029. — Bernama

Title: ‘Come once a week’: Selangor Sultan asks Ronnie Liu, DAP rep to visit Rukun Negara monument over pig farming controversy
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Sultan of Selangor Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah has called on all Selangor assemblymen to visit Dataran Selangor twice a month to read and understand the Rukun Negara displayed on a newly built monument plaque.
In a statement today, His Royal Highness said the reminder was especially directed at Seri Kembangan assemblyman Wong Siew Ki of DAP and former Selangor executive councillor Ronnie Liu Tian Khiew.
“I suggest that they come once a week to Dataran Selangor to understand and appreciate the meaning of the Rukun Negara, so that they become citizens who know how to respect the sovereignty of the royal institution and uphold courtesy and morality,” Sultan Sharafuddin said.
His Royal Highness said the plaque, placed in front of the Selangor state flagpole, was built and installed by the Selangor Public Works Department together with benches for visitors.
Sultan Sharafuddin said he had fully funded the cost of the Rukun Negara monument plaque and the benches.
His Royal Highness said the monument was meant to remind Malaysians, especially the younger generation, of the history behind the Rukun Negara, which was introduced after the May 13, 1969 racial riots and the declaration of a state of emergency.
The royal reminder comes after recent debate over Selangor’s proposed large-scale pig farming project in Bukit Tagar, after Sultan Sharafuddin said he did not consent to pig farming being carried out in any district of Selangor, citing environmental concerns, limited land resources and public well-being.
In January, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said he would consult Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari on a proposal to cancel the Bukit Tagar project and consider relocating it to a more suitable site.
Anwar later said the government did not prohibit pig farming, but such farms should not be located in areas that could cause public concern, including near residential areas.

Title: Sabah probes suspected ivory poaching after mutilated Bornean elephant found in Tongod forest reserve
Article:
KOTA KINABALU, May 19 — Sabah authorities are investigating the death of a Bornean elephant found mutilated in a forest reserve in Tongod, believed to be linked to ivory poaching.
State Tourism, Culture and Environment Minister Datuk Jafry Ariffin said he was deeply concerned by the discovery of the dead elephant within the nursery forest area of the Sungai Pinangah Forest Reserve.
According to reports, the Kinabatangan Wildlife team received a complaint at approximately 8.25am on May 18, 2026 regarding a dead elephant suspected to be linked to wildlife crime.
A field inspection was conducted the same day at 12.13pm at the incident site within the nursery forest area in Sungai Pinangah Forest Reserve, Tongod.
A male Bornean elephant (Elephas maximus borneensis), measuring approximately 2.9 metres in height with a footprint size of 1.45 metres, was found with its face and mouth cut in an L-shaped pattern using sharp tools, while the tusks were missing and suspected to have been removed.
Initial inspections also found cut marks on the head area.
According to investigations, estate staff in Tongod had on May 17 at about 5.30pm heard a loud explosion-like sound. However, inspection efforts could not be carried out immediately due to continuous heavy rainfall throughout the night.
At 6.30am the following morning, the estate manager together with staff conducted monitoring activities and discovered the dead elephant.
At this stage, no gunshot wounds were detected on the body parts examined, although a full examination of the animal is still ongoing. Heavy rainfall during the suspected period also made it difficult for investigators to identify tracks and evidence at the scene.
“The exact cause of death has yet to be determined and a post-mortem examination together with further investigations will now be carried out by the Sabah Wildlife Department,” said Jafry.
“I wish to stress that if investigations confirm the involvement of poaching activities, illegal ivory removal or other wildlife offences, firm action without compromise must be taken against those responsible,” he said.
“The Bornean elephant is one of Sabah’s most iconic and protected wildlife species and remains an important component of the state’s biodiversity and conservation efforts. Any act threatening its survival must be treated seriously.
“We cannot allow Sabah to become a ground for wildlife crime. Every loss of a protected species is a loss to our natural heritage and the future of Sabah’s biodiversity.”

Title: Amirudin calls for grassroots cooperation to curb cable theft disrupting digital services in Selangor
Article:
SHAH ALAM, May 19 — Local communities have been urged to play a more active role in providing early information to help authorities combat cable theft and vandalism involving telecommunications infrastructure in Selangor.
Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari said public cooperation is crucial to ensure immediate action can be taken to prevent acts of sabotage, which not only damage public facilities but also disrupt the public’s digital access.
“We (the state government) want the public to be involved so that cable theft activities, including vandalism against digital facilities, can be reduced. As I mentioned, digital infrastructure is important for sustaining daily life in the state of Selangor.
“Integrated measures mobilised at the grassroots level are crucial to ensuring that every piece of the state’s digital infrastructure is continuously protected from sabotage threats,” he said after launching the state-level Telecommunications Infrastructure Vandalism Prevention Campaign 2026 here today.
Amirudin said such efforts need to be strengthened to ensure continued protection of the state’s digital infrastructure, following a high number of incidents recorded over the past year.
According to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), Selangor remains among the states recording the highest number of cases in Malaysia, with 1,836 cases reported in 2024, followed by 1,273 cases last year.
MCMC senior advisor (Operations) Datuk Zurkarnain Mohd Yasin, who was also present at the launch, said the impact of physical damage to infrastructure has been significant, with the telecommunications industry forced to bear losses exceeding RM20 million annually for repair works.
Several areas in Selangor have also been identified as high-risk locations experiencing repeated incidents, namely Rawang, Gombak, Bangi, the Sungai Tekali area and Hulu Langat, where service disruption periods are longer, involving critical components such as microwave transmission and certain network equipment.
“Spare parts need to be ordered from overseas, and the delivery and reinstallation process requires an additional time of between one and three years before services can be fully restored. This situation directly affects the stability of telecommunications services and impacts users and communities,” he said.
He added that his team is actively working to ensure more effective enforcement actions by strengthening prevention strategies through engagement sessions in hotspot areas.
Such an approach, he said, can foster closer cooperation with various parties, including the Royal Malaysia Police, village heads, community leaders, residents’ associations and local communities in efforts to raise awareness and encourage the reporting of suspicious activities to authorities. — Bernama

Title: Penang 75pc ready for snap polls, says Chow Kon Yeow
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, May 19 — Penang is about 75 per cent ready to face snap polls, said Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow.
He said there is no fixed point at which a state can be considered fully ready for elections, whether at 80 or 100 per cent.
““If you ask me, I always give the answer 75 per cent, not completely prepared, but educated prepared if there is a possibility,” he said when he was asked whether the state is ready to face snap polls.
“When parliament is dissolved, it’s a sign to be ready,” he added.
As for whether Pakatan Harapan Penang will continue to collaborate with Barisan Nasional in the elections, he said it is still open for them to decide later.
Chow, who is Penang PH chairman, said some states plan to go solo while some states viewed the cooperation under the Unity coalition as still relevant.
“Ultimately, the decision comes from the leadership at the national level,” he said.
He said all states will have to align themselves with the national leadership’s decision.
Earlier, Chow announced that the state has completed 60 per cent or 30 items under the MyPenang Manifesto.
He said 17 items or 34 per cent are being implemented while only three items or six per cent are yet to be implemented.
He said the manifesto achievement announcement coincided with the recent statement by the Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim that snap polls can be called anytime.
“We normally give updates on the manifesto achievements so regardless of when elections are held, work to complete the manifesto continues,” he said.
He said if elections are called, possibly some of the manifesto items may not be completed in time.
He said the public can access the MyPenang Manifesto dashboard online to check on the progress of the projects being implemented.

Title: Zambry: BN will stay with unity govt until end of mandate
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, May 19 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will continue to preserve understanding in the Unity Government until the end of the administration’s mandate to ensure national stability and the well-being of the people, said its secretary-general Datuk Seri Zambry Abd Kadir.
He said BN is of the view that the understanding and basis for the formation of the Unity Government after the 15th general election (GE15) is the responsibility of the nation to prevent Malaysia from falling into prolonged political polemics.
“The understanding was agreed upon until the mandate ends. Therefore, there is no question of Barisan Nasional betraying the Unity Government,” he said in a statement here, today.
